Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 41 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 360 upvotes, 86 comments
  3. An Example of Statistical Investigation of the Text Eugene Onegin – Markov, 1913 [pdf] 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had 136 upvotes, 31 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 767 upvotes, 226 comments
  6. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 319 upvotes, 93 comments
  7. Hugo's New CSS Powers 23 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 335 upvotes, 117 comments
  9. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 32 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. George Goble died recently – known for first dual-CPU-Unix and fast BBQ lighting 56 upvotes, 14 comments
  11. LinkedIn is illegally searching your computer 1391 upvotes, 617 comments
  12. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 220 upvotes, 89 comments
  13. Tailscale's new macOS home 144 upvotes, 55 comments
  14. Yggdrasil Network 50 upvotes, 15 comments
  15. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 260 upvotes, 117 comments
  16. JSON Canvas Spec 46 upvotes, 15 comments
  17. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 652 upvotes, 341 comments
  18. Renewables reached nearly 50% of global electricity capacity last year 187 upvotes, 87 comments
  19. Delve allegedly forked an open-source tool and sold it as its own 226 upvotes, 111 comments
  20. Significant Raise of Reports 243 upvotes, 125 comments
  21. Quadratic Micropass Type Inference 22 upvotes, 6 comments
  22. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 136 upvotes, 68 comments
  23. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 178 upvotes, 99 comments
  24. Magic the Gathering Deck Shuffler 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  25. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 246 upvotes, 156 comments
  26. Cursor 3 93 upvotes, 86 comments
  27. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 62 upvotes, 59 comments
  28. We sped up bun by 100x 39 upvotes, 35 comments
  29. The case for zero-error horizons in trustworthy LLMs 52 upvotes, 77 comments
  30. Zep AI Is Hiring – Building the Agent Context Layer (YC W24) 0 upvotes, 0 comments
